column3: Description
Name of the supply chain (SC) node
Unique identifier for the node (alphanumeric)
Type/category of the SC node (e.g., manufacturer, distributor)
Geographic location (latitude, longitude) of the node
Whether the node has inventory storage capability
Type of inventory (e.g., finished goods, raw materials)
Replenishment policy (e.g., periodic, EOQ)
Maximum inventory level that can be stored
Threshold to trigger replenishment
Cost incurred per unit for holding inventory
Time interval for placing replenishment orders
List of products that are stored in the inventory
Cost incurred per order placed to the supplier to replenish the inventory
Cost to produce a unit/batch of product (node-specific attribute)
Cost of setting up the node (building the production plant or warehouse)
Decrease in product value over time due to wear, tear, or unfavorable market conditions
Penalty cost for delayed orders
Description
Number of product units sold per unit of time (day/month/quarter)
Total number of items sold over a given time period
Net profit generated over a given time period (current month/quarter)
Average net profit per time unit (day/month/quarter)
Inventory levels at regular time intervals (daily/monthly)
Total node cost (includes manufacturing, storing, moving, loading, unloading, and transporting)
Average node cost per unit time
Number of orders (and product quantity) placed by this node
Number of orders (and product quantity) received at this node
Number of orders (and product quantity) fulfilled for this node
Number of orders fulfilled by this node
Percentage of demand fulfilled
Number of orders not fulfilled by this node due to unavailability
Inventory waste due to product expiration (applicable for perishable products)
Cost of storing each product item at the node
Expenses for replenishing the inventory
Transportation cost paid for replenishment orders by this node
Total income generated by this node
Quantity of goods produced in a given time period
Probability of reliable supply at the node
Node's ability to recover from disruption
Refers to the importance of the node (e.g., a node that could cut the main flow of product if it is down)
Shortage amount (unmet demand quantity)
